name: Baked Trout with Yoghurt and Herbs
recipeIngredient: 7 ½ fl oz Greek strained yoghurt
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose a baking dish or other shallow oven‑proof dish that is large enough to hold the trout in a single layer, head to tail.
2. Dry the fish thoroughly with plenty of kitchen paper.
3. Heat a frying pan very hot and briefly fry the fish—about one or two pieces at a time—until both sides are well browned.
4. Transfer the fish neatly to the oven‑proof dish and cover; set aside in a cool place.
5. Chop the herbs (parsley, chives, fennel, thyme, oregano, tarragon) and gently mix them in a small saucepan.
6. Measure the yoghurt and whipping cream, pour over the herbs, add plenty of pepper and a pinch of salt, then stir well to combine. Cover; allow to infuse for several hours (or overnight).
7. About ½ hour before serving, slowly bring the yoghurt‑herb mixture to a simmer while stirring.
8. Pour the aromatic sauce over the fish, return the dish to the oven, cover, and bake at 350 °F (180 °C) for 20–25 minutes until the trout are hot and fully cooked through. Check doneness by inserting a knife into the flesh behind the head; it should be clean.
9. Serve with steamed new potatoes and other young summer vegetables such as carrots and peas.
(Note: The recipe can be prepared ahead, but ensure both the saucepan and dish reach room temperature about 1 hour before baking.)
